Source Note: This is a compilation of references by the same team, where brain and body mass data taken from the same individuals with the same definitions (by default)

Definition: O/N, ratio between numbers of other (non-neuronal) cells and neurons
whole cerebellum (both brain halves), including the deep nuclei
sum of gray matter, subcortical white matter, and including the hippocampus, amygdala and piriform cortex in the two hemispheres
Rest of brain; the ensemble of brainstem, diencephalon and basal ganglia, for both sides of the brain; olfactory tract included
whole brain (both sides), not including the olfactory bulbs
Note: "Brain mass, g" refers to "Whole brain" as defined elsewhere

Definition: number of neurons / (number of neurons + number of other cells) * 100
